title: Missing access control on init function allows setting malicious oracle
reason: `init()` is the intended clone initializer invoked during `BentoBox.deploy`; no concrete path was shown to hijack already deployed funded markets, and initializing the master contract itself does not compromise existing clones.

title: Reentrancy vulnerability in liquidate function via arbitrary swapper
reason: The swapper callback occurs before the final MIM repayment transfer, and any failure to repay reverts the entire liquidation; no concrete reentrant path to steal collateral or corrupt accounting was validated.

title: No slippage protection in liquidation swap
reason: Open liquidation intentionally lets the caller choose `to` and `swapper`; the liquidator bears that execution risk, so this is not a protocol vulnerability.

title: Oracle price not validated for reasonableness
reason: This is a generic oracle-trust observation rather than a distinct code flaw; the concrete reportable oracle issues here are the stale/zero cached-rate behaviors already captured separately.

title: Open liquidation allows front-running and MEV extraction
reason: Permissionless liquidation and MEV competition are expected market mechanics, not a code-level vulnerability.

title: Potential integer overflow in accrue interest calculation
reason: The arithmetic is checked, and the described overflow requires extreme/unrealistic values or time horizons, making it non-practical as a protocol risk.

title: Missing event for critical reduceSupply function
reason: Informational only; it does not create realistic protocol-level harm.

title: No access control on setFeeTo function
reason: `setFeeTo()` is protected by `onlyOwner`; setting a poor recipient is an authorized governance choice, not missing access control.
